reviews.verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED before git. All four known roadmap issues are CONFIRMED and were extended across the tree. (1) Loop/Subworkflow is a genuine forward-reference gap — RESERVE both (forward-compat enum only; drop LoopNode from the Phase-1 canvas build and Subworkflow stays unbuilt), because an iterating LoopNode also collides with Phase 1's 'cycle = hard error' rule. (2) The node-type NAME taxonomy reconciles correctly in node-types.md, but two config-field VALUE vocabularies (merge_strategy, timeout/on_timeout) are NOT reconciled there and drift across docs. (3) The Phase-3 '3.M' milestone prefix collides with workstream '3.M' — renumber to 3.M1–3.M5. (4) The gate-paused exit code is unpinned in BOTH commands.md and the roadmap, and is named three different ways — pin it to 3 and standardize on 'gate-paused'. Beyond the known four: a trigger-taxonomy drift (mcp_call dropped; webhook/schedule mislabeled 'reserved'), a pnpm --filter package-name inconsistency that breaks the day-one setup runbook, an unowned @relavium/db build (required in Phase 2 but scoped out of Phase 1 and absent from the build-order spine), and a likely broken architectural-principles.md link cited 4x from guide-layer docs. None of these are blocking-severe individually, but the Loop scope decision, the exit-code pin, and the 3.M renumber should land before the docs are committed since later phases build directly on them.

plan.taxonomyDecision:
CANONICAL TAXONOMY (single source of truth = reference/shared-core/node-types.md; every other doc defers to it and must not re-define the mapping). The three layers legitimately have three different counts; that is correct and must be stated as intentional, not "drift":

1) AUTHORED YAML `type` — 8 values: input, agent, human_gate, condition, transform, parallel, merge, output. This is the user contract (workflow-yaml-spec.md v1.0). A Tool is authored via an agent node's `tools:` list, NOT as its own YAML type; loop and subworkflow are NOT authorable in v1.0 (reserved).
2) CANVAS components — 9 React node components: AgentNode, ConditionNode, FanOutNode, AggregatorNode, LoopNode, HumanGateNode, InputNode, OutputNode, ToolNode. (9 because the canvas adds a visual ToolNode and a LoopNode; ToolNode serializes into an agent's tools, and LoopNode is reserved/non-authorable in v1.0 — see loop decision.)
3) ENGINE enum — 11 internal types: agent | condition | tool | transform | input | output | loop | fan_out | fan_in | human_in_the_loop | subworkflow. The single authored `parallel` expands into TWO engine vertices (fan_out + fan_in); human_gate maps to human_in_the_loop; loop and subworkflow exist in the enum but have no v1.0 YAML `type`.

CANONICAL NAME MAPPINGS (only stated once, in node-types.md): parallel(YAML) -> FanOutNode(canvas) -> fan_out+fan_in(engine); merge(YAML) -> AggregatorNode(canvas) -> fan_in(engine); human_gate(YAML) -> HumanGateNode(canvas) -> human_in_the_loop(engine); tool -> ToolNode(canvas)/agent tools: (YAML) -> tool(engine).

THE ONE-LINE RULE every doc must follow: "The authored YAML `type` is the authoritative value (8 in v1.0); canvas component names and the engine enum are renderings of it, reconciled only in reference/shared-core/node-types.md — never re-enumerate or rename node types elsewhere; link to node-types.md instead." Counts are reconciled as: 8 YAML types / 9 canvas components / 11 engine enum members — and each doc must use the count appropriate to the layer it is describing and cite node-types.md.
plan.loopSubworkflowDecision:
RESERVE (forward-compat; not executable, not authorable, and not user-functional on the canvas in the first increment). Rationale: every load-bearing layer already reserves them (Phase 0 NodeSchema union = 8 types with no loop/subworkflow; workflow-yaml-spec.md v1.0 marks them reserved; node-types.md marks YAML `type` "(reserved)"; Phase 1 1.P scopes handlers only for condition/fan_out/fan_in/transform/input/output, plus agent in 1.O and human gate in 1.Q) — so RESERVE is the choice that requires the fewest changes, preserves MVP minimalism, and keeps the engine enum/canvas component as honest forward-compat slots. Building now would force new scope into Phase 0 schema, Phase 1 1.P, YAML v1.0, and a loop/subworkflow conformance burden for no MVP user value.

DOCS THAT MUST STATE THE RESERVE DECISION CONSISTENTLY:
- reference/shared-core/node-types.md — already correct (keeps YAML `type` "(reserved)" for loop/subworkflow); add an explicit "Reserved (not executable / not authorable in v1.0)" note so LoopNode's prose stops reading as fully shipped, and keep `loop`/`subworkflow` in the engine enum as forward-compat-only.
- reference/contracts/workflow-yaml-spec.md — already correct (8 authorable types; reserved note); no change beyond confirming the reserved framing matches node-types.md.
- glossary.md — MUST change: LoopNode is currently defined as executable ("repeats a sub-graph until a condition is met") and counted in the shippable canvas set; re-label it reserved/forward-compat (not authorable in YAML v1.0, not runnable in Phase 1).
- roadmap/phases/phase-3-desktop.md — MUST change: 3.E ("nine custom node components"), 3.F ("the LoopNode iteration badge ... from the canonical event stream"), and 3.M-canvas ("all 9 node types") imply a functional LoopNode. Mark LoopNode as a reserved/disabled palette item (rendered for forward-compat but not runnable/serializable) OR drop it from the Phase-3 functional canvas set; remove the live LoopNode iteration-badge claim from 3.F since no engine event drives it in Phase 1.
- roadmap/phases/phase-0-foundations.md (0.E) and phase-1-engine-and-llm.md (1.P) — no functional change, but add a one-line "loop/subworkflow are reserved enum/schema slots, no v1.0 handler" note so the omission is intentional and visible, not an apparent gap.

crossCuttingThemes: One-canonical-home is the cure for almost every finding: node types belong only in reference/shared-core/node-types.md, the exit-code table only in reference/cli/commands.md, the adapter set only in ADR-0011/tech-stack — most conflicts come from a second doc re-enumerating instead of linking. Enforce 'link, do not re-list.'

Forward-compat slots (loop, subworkflow) must be labeled 'reserved / not executable in v1.0' EVERYWHERE they appear, or they silently read as shipped features at whichever layer the reader enters first (glossary vs schema vs canvas). One reserve decision, stated identically across product/schema/engine/canvas.

Layer-appropriate counts: 8 YAML types / 9 canvas components / 11 engine enum members are all correct for their layer; the fix is to always pair a count with its layer and a node-types.md citation, not to force a single number.

Two docs that each defer the decision to the other = an unresolved decision (the gate-paused exit code: commands.md says 'phase-2 will lock it', phase-2 says 'lock it in commands.md'). A value or invariant must be pinned in exactly one home and referenced from the other.

Identifier-namespace hygiene: in-phase milestone ids must not collide with workstream ids or the global M-spine (3.M workstream vs 3.M-* milestone vs global M3/M4). Prefix in-phase milestones distinctly (P3.M1..) across all phases.

Nuance loss in the product layer: binding/product docs (product-constraints, glossary) flatten 'declarable in P1 / auto-fire in P2' into a flat 'Phase 2', losing a distinction the reference spec makes — product-layer summaries should preserve, not collapse, the reference nuance.

plan.overallReadiness: The docs are close to git-ready: the four known cross-document issues are all genuine and now have concrete, low-risk resolutions, and no new structural contradictions surfaced beyond a stray fourth adapter / RateLimiter in project-structure.md and a one-word gate-pending/gate-paused drift inside commands.md. Once the high-priority fixes land (reserve-label Loop/Subworkflow consistently across glossary + node-types + phase-3, pin the gate-paused exit code to 3 in both commands.md and phase-2, and renumber the colliding 3.M milestones), the tree will be internally consistent and a reader entering at any layer will not be misled. The medium/low fixes (adapter count, trigger-phasing nuance, architecture inline enumeration) are correctness polish that should ship in the same pass but do not block git on their own.
